Name: Pineco
Flavor-Text: It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn’t bother it.

Name: Forretress ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 5 Basic [G] Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck. If you searched your deck in this way, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Maractus
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Once each year, this Pokémon scatters its seeds. They’re jam-packed with nutrients, making them a precious food source out in the desert.

Name: Toedscool
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

Name: Toedscruel ex
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent’s Pokémon done to all of your Pokémon that have Energy attached. (Existing effects are not removed. Damage is not an effect.)
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any [G] Energy attached.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Espathra ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent’s Active Pokémon cost [C] more.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Charmander
Effects: Discard a Stadium in play.
Flavor-Text: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

Name: Charmeleon
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent’s Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)
Flavor-Text: If it becomes agitated during battle, it spouts intense flames, incinerating its surroundings.

Name: Magmar
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon’s bodies are constantly burning. Magmar are feared as one of the causes behind fires.

Name: Magmortar
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Living in the crater of a volcano has caused this Pokémon’s body to resemble its environment— it has an organ similar to a magma chamber.

Name: Numel
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Magma of almost 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit courses through its body. When it grows cold, the magma hardens and slows it.

Name: Camerupt
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
You may discard a [F] Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives in the crater of a volcano. It is well known that the humps on its back erupt every 10 years.

Name: Heat Rotom
Effects: This Pokémon also does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: If the convection microwave oven is not working properly, then the Rotom inhabiting it will become lethargic.

Name: Charcadet
Flavor-Text: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

Name: Armarouge
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a [R] Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.

Name: Lapras
Effects: Put up to 3 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Crossing icy seas is no issue for this cold-resistant Pokémon. Its smooth skin is a little cool to the touch.

Name: Frigibax
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Frigibax absorbs heat through its dorsal fin and converts the heat into ice energy. The higher the temperature, the more energy Frigibax stores.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: ﻿When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

Name: Raichu
Ability-Effect: When 1 of your Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, you may move a [L] Energy from that Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

Name: Chinchou
Effects: Look at the top card of your deck. You may put that card on the bottom of your deck.
Flavor-Text: On the dark ocean floor, its only means of communication is its constantly flashing lights.

Name: Lanturn
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [W] Energy attached, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon flashes a bright light that blinds its prey. This creates an opening for it to deliver an electrical attack.

Name: Kilowattrel
Effects: This attack does 10 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the United Wings attack to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.

Name: Exeggcute
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon get nervous when they’re not in a group of six. The minute even one member of the group goes missing, Exeggcute become cowardly.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Put 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: When they work together, Exeggutor’s three heads can put out powerful psychic energy. Cloudy days make this Pokémon sluggish.

Name: Natu
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Because its wings aren’t yet fully grown, it has to hop to get around. It is always staring at something.

Name: Xatu
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic [P] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: This odd Pokémon can see both the past and the future. It eyes the sun’s movement all day.

Name: Ralts
Flavor-Text: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people’s emotions.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

Name: Gardevoir ex
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic [P] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your [P] Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon. You can’t use this Ability on a Pokémon that would be Knocked Out.
Effects: This Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Chimecho
Effects: Switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot. The new Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Emitting ultrasonic cries, it floats on winds to travel great distances.

Name: Mime Jr.
Effects: Your opponent chooses an attack from 1 of their Pokémon in play. Use the chosen attack as this attack.
Flavor-Text: In an attempt to confuse its enemy, it mimics the enemy’s movements. Then it wastes no time in making itself scarce!

Name: Woobat
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It emits ultrasonic waves as it flutters about, searching for its prey—bug Pokémon.

Name: Swoobat
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused. Put 6 damage counters instead of 3 on that Pokémon for this Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: The auspicious shape of this Pokémon’s nose apparently led some regions to consider Swoobat a symbol of good luck.

Name: Cottonee
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Weaving together the cotton of both Cottonee and Eldegoss produces exquisite cloth that’s highly prized by many luxury brands.

Name: Whimsicott
Flavor-Text: As long as this Pokémon bathes in sunlight, its cotton keeps growing. If too much cotton fluff builds up, Whimsicott tears it off and scatters it.

Name: Dedenne
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It’s small and its electricity-generating organ is not fully developed, so it uses its tail to absorb electricity from people’s homes and charge itself.

Name: Mimikyu
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon ex and Pokémon V.
Effects: Put 7 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in dark places untouched by sunlight. When it appears before humans, it hides itself under a cloth that resembles a Pikachu.

Name: Fidough
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is smooth and moist to the touch. Yeast in Fidough’s breath induces fermentation in the Pokémon’s vicinity.

Name: Dachsbun
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t be Burned. Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s [R] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The pleasant aroma that emanates from this Pokémon’s body helps wheat grow, so Dachsbun has been treasured by farming villages.

Name: Ceruledge
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The fiery blades on its arms burn fiercely with the lingering resentment of a sword wielder who fell before accomplishing their goal.

Name: Flittle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Flittle’s toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon’s belly.

Name: Greavard
Effects: This attack does 10 damage for each [P] Pokémon in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: This friendly Pokémon doesn’t like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.

Name: Houndstone
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each [P] Pokémon in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: A lovingly mourned Pokémon was reborn as Houndstone. It doesn’t like anyone touching the protuberance atop its head.

Name: Gimmighoul
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was born inside a treasure chest about 1,500 years ago. It sucks the life-force out of scoundrels who try to steal the treasure.

Name: Mankey
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It is extremely quick to anger. It could be docile one moment, then thrashing away the next instant.

Name: Primeape
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Some researchers theorize that Primeape remains angry even when inside a Poké Ball.

Name: Annihilape
Effects: This attack does 70 damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It imbues its fists with the power of the rage that it kept hidden in its heart. Opponents struck by these imbued fists will be shattered to their core.

Name: Phanpy
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives and nests on a riverbank. After playing in the mud, it won’t be able to settle down unless it washes its body.

Name: Donphan
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: Donphan is covered in tough hide, so even being hit by a car won’t faze this Pokémon. However, it is extremely susceptible to rain.

Name: Barboach
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its two whiskers provide a sensitive radar. Even in muddy waters, it can detect its prey’s location.

Name: Clobbopus
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Its tentacles tear off easily, but it isn’t alarmed when that happens—it knows they’ll grow back. It’s about as smart as a three-year-old.

Name: Grapploct
Effects: At the end of your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon will be Knocked Out.
Flavor-Text: Searching for an opponent to test its skills against, it emerges onto land. Once the battle is over, it returns to the sea.

Name: Great Tusk ex
Ability-Effect: Once at the end of your turn (after your attack), if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you must discard the top 5 cards of your deck.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Charizard ex
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 3 Basic [R] Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Gastly
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It wraps its opponent in its gas-like body, slowly weakening its prey by poisoning it through the skin.

Name: Haunter
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

Name: Gengar
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: To steal the life of its target, it slips into the prey’s shadow and silently waits for an opportunity.

Name: Paldean Wooper
Effects: Search your deck for a Stadium card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: After losing a territorial struggle, Wooper began living on land. The Pokémon changed over time, developing a poisonous film to protect its body.

Name: Paldean Clodsire ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if a Stadium is in play, you may make your opponent’s Active Pokémon Poisoned.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Scraggy
Flavor-Text: It protects itself with its durable skin. It’s thought that this Pokémon will evolve once its skin has completely stretched out.

Name: Scrafty
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: While mostly known for having the temperament of an aggressive ruffian, this Pokémon takes very good care of its family, friends, and territory.

Name: Maschiff
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It always scowls in an attempt to make opponents take it seriously, but even crying children will burst into laughter when they see Maschiff’s face.

Name: Mabosstiff
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Mabosstiff loves playing with children. Though usually gentle, it takes on an intimidating look when protecting its family.

Name: Varoom
Effects: Put up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.

Name: Revavroom
Ability-Effect: You must discard an Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders— now numbering eight—to generate energy.

Name: Iron Treads ex
Effects: After doing damage, you may discard all Future Booster Energy Capsules from this Pokémon. If you do, during your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 150 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gholdengo
Effects: You may attach any number of Basic [M] Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Flip a coin for each [M] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its body seems to be made up of 1,000 coins. This Pokémon gets along well with others and is quick to make friends with anybody.

Name: Noibat
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon emits ultrasonic waves from its large ears to search for fruit to eat. It mistakes Applin for its food.

Name: Noivern ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Special Energy or Stadium cards from their hand.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Cyclizar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Apparently Cyclizar has been allowing people to ride on its back since ancient times. Depictions of this have been found in 10,000-year-old murals.

Name: Lechonk
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn’t use it for anything other than foraging.

Name: Oinkologne
Effects: Flip 3 coins. Put a number of cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile into your hand.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon sends a flowerlike scent wafting about. Well-developed muscles in its legs allow it to leap more than 16 feet with no trouble at all.

Name: Tandemaus
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: Exhibiting great teamwork, they use their incisors to cut pieces out of any material that might be useful for a nest, then make off with them.

Name: Maushold
Effects: This attack does 70 damage for each of your Maushold in play.
Flavor-Text: The larger pair protects the little ones during battles. When facing strong opponents, the whole group will join the fight.

Name: Squawkabilly ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your first turn, you may discard your hand and draw 6 cards. You can’t use more than 1 Squawk and Seize Ability during your turn.
Effects: Attach up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Artazon
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may search their deck for a Basic Pokémon that doesn’t have a Rule Box and put it onto their Bench. Then, that player shuffles their deck. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Atticus
Description: You can use this card only if your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned.Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 7 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Clive
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you draw 2 cards for each Supporter card you find there.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Electric Generator
Description: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and attach up to 2 Basic [L] Energy cards you find there to your Benched [L] Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Iono
Description: Each player shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If either player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, each player draws a card for each of their remaining Prize cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Moonlit Hill
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may discard a Basic [P] Energy card from their hand in order to heal 30 damage from each of their Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

Name: Nemona
Description: Draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Nemona’s Backpack
Description: Put up to 2 Nemona cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Nest Ball
Description: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Paldean Student
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon that doesn’t have a Rule Box, reveal it, and put it into your hand. For each Paldean Student card (not including this card) in your discard pile, you may search for an additional Pokémon in this way. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Paldean Student
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon that doesn’t have a Rule Box, reveal it, and put it into your hand. For each Paldean Student card (not including this card) in your discard pile, you may search for an additional Pokémon in this way. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Professor’s Research
Description: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Professor’s Research
Description: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Rare Candy
Description: Choose 1 of your Basic Pokémon in play. If you have a Stage 2 card in your hand that evolves from that Pokémon, put that card onto the Basic Pokémon to evolve it, skipping the Stage 1. You can’t use this card during your first turn or on a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Technical Machine: Crisis Punch
Effects: You can use this attack only when your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining.
Description: The Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.) If this card is attached to 1 of your Pokémon, discard it at the end of your turn.
Tool-Rule: You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

Name: Ultra Ball
Description: You can use this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Oddish
Flavor-Text: During the day, it stays in the cold underground to avoid the sun. It grows by bathing in moonlight.

Name: Gloom
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may look at the top 3 cards of your deck and attach any number of Basic Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: What appears to be drool is actually sweet honey. It is very sticky and clings stubbornly if touched.

Name: Vileplume
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may look at the top 8 cards of your deck and attach any number of Basic Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Flavor-Text: The larger its petals, the more toxic pollen it contains. Its big head is heavy and hard to hold up.

Name: Scyther
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It slashes through grass with its sharp scythes, moving too fast for the human eye to track.

Name: Hoppip
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is blown across vast distances by the wind. It is unclear where the Hoppip of Paldea originally came from.

Name: Skiploom
Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Skiploom enthusiasts can apparently tell where a Skiploom was born by the scent drifting from the flower on the Pokémon’s head.

Name: Jumpluff
Ability-Effect: If any damage is done to this Pokémon by attacks, flip a coin. If heads, prevent that damage.
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Jumpluff travels on seasonal winds. Once its cotton spores run out, its journey ends, as does its life.

Name: Pineco
Flavor-Text: It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn’t bother it.

Name: Snover
Flavor-Text: During cold seasons, it migrates to the mountain’s lower reaches. It returns to the snow-covered summit in the spring.

Name: Abomasnow
Ability-Effect: Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) can’t be healed.
Flavor-Text: It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.

Name: Smoliv
Effects: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It protects itself from enemies by emitting oil from the fruit on its head. This oil is bitter and astringent enough to make someone flinch.

Name: Dolliv
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn’t happen.
Flavor-Text: Dolliv shares its tasty, fresh-scented oil with others. This species has coexisted with humans since times long gone.

Name: Arboliva
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This calm Pokémon is very compassionate. It will share its delicious, nutrient-rich oil with weakened Pokémon.

Name: Toedscool
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Though it looks like Tentacool, Toedscool is a completely different species. Its legs may be thin, but it can run at a speed of 30 mph.

Name: Capsakid
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic [R] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Traditional Paldean dishes can be extremely spicy because they include the shed front teeth of Capsakid among their ingredients.

Name: Scovillain
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in play, it is [G] and [R] type.
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: The red head converts spicy chemicals into fire energy and blasts the surrounding area with a super spicy stream of flame.

Name: Rellor
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon creates a mud ball by mixing sand and dirt with psychic energy. It treasures its mud ball more than its own life.

Name: Charmander
Effects: Discard a Stadium in play.
Flavor-Text: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.

Name: Charmeleon
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent’s Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)
Flavor-Text: If it becomes agitated during battle, it spouts intense flames, incinerating its surroundings.

Name: Paldean Tauros
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: People call this kind of Tauros the Blaze Breed due to the hot air it snorts from its nostrils. Its three tails are intertwined.

Name: Entei
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent’s Active Pokémon do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said that when it roars, a volcano erupts somewhere around the globe.

Name: Oricorio
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from your Active Evolution Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is incredibly popular, possibly because its passionate dancing is a great match with the temperament of Paldean people.

Name: Charcadet
Flavor-Text: Burnt charcoal came to life and became a Pokémon. Possessing a fiery fighting spirit, Charcadet will battle even tough opponents.

Name: Armarouge
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a [R] Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your Active Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: Armarouge evolved through the use of a set of armor that belonged to a distinguished warrior. This Pokémon is incredibly loyal.

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal 30 damage from it.
Flavor-Text: It is always vacantly lost in thought, but no one knows what it is thinking about. It is good at fishing with its tail.

Name: Slowbro
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If the tail-biting Shellder is thrown off in a harsh battle, this Pokémon reverts to being an ordinary Slowpoke.

Name: Staryu
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Fish Pokémon nibble at it, but Staryu isn’t bothered. Its body regenerates quickly, even if part of it is completely torn off.

Name: Starmie
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. If you placed any damage counters in this way, discard this Pokémon and all attached cards.
Flavor-Text: Starmie swims by spinning its body at high speed. As this Pokémon cruises through the ocean, it absorbs tiny plankton.

Name: Paldean Tauros
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It swims by jetting water from its horns. The most notable characteristic of the Aqua Breed is its high body fat, which allows it to float easily.

Name: Wiglett
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.

Name: Wugtrio
Effects: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, discard the top 3 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It has a vicious temperament, contrary to what its appearance may suggest. It wraps its long bodies around prey, then drags the prey into its den.

Name: Finizen
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It likes playing with others of its kind using the water ring on its tail. It uses ultrasonic waves to sense the emotions of other living creatures.

Name: Palafin
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If this Pokémon didn’t move from the Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s ancient genes have awakened. It is now so extraordinarily strong that it can easily lift a cruise ship with one fin.

Name: Veluza
Effects: If you have no cards in your hand, this attack can be used for [W].
Flavor-Text: When Veluza discards unnecessary flesh, its mind becomes honed and its psychic power increases. The spare flesh has a mild but delicious flavor.

Name: Dondozo
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each Tatsugiri in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is a glutton, but it’s bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.

Name: Tatsugiri
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic [W] Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Basic Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
Flavor-Text: This is a small dragon Pokémon. It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.

Name: Frigibax
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: Frigibax absorbs heat through its dorsal fin and converts the heat into ice energy. The higher the temperature, the more energy Frigibax stores.

Name: Arctibax
Flavor-Text: Arctibax freezes the air around it, protecting its face with an ice mask and turning its dorsal fin into a blade of ice.

Name: Baxcalibur
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic [W] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon blasts cryogenic air out from its mouth. This air can instantly freeze even liquid-hot lava.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: ﻿When it is angered, it immediately discharges the energy stored in the pouches in its cheeks.

Name: Raichu
Ability-Effect: When 1 of your Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, you may move a [L] Energy from that Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.

Name: Voltorb
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.

Name: Electrode
Effects: Before doing damage, you may discard any number of Pokémon Tools from your Pokémon. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.

Name: Shinx
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The extension and contraction of its muscles generates electricity. It glows when in trouble.

Name: Luxio
Flavor-Text: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

Name: Luxray
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in your hand and you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, you may put this Pokémon onto your Bench.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It can see clearly through walls to track down its prey and seek its lost young.

Name: Pachirisu
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t be Paralyzed.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each of your Benched [L] Pokémon. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness.
Flavor-Text: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

Name: Thundurus
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 90 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: As it flies around, it shoots lightning all over the place and causes forest fires. It is therefore disliked.

Name: Toxel
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It has no problem drinking dirty water. An organ inside Toxel’s body filters such water into a poisonous liquid that is harmless to Toxel.

Name: Toxtricity
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This attack does 30 more damage for each different type of Pokémon on your Bench.
Flavor-Text: Many youths admire the way this Pokémon listlessly picks fights and keeps its cool no matter what opponent it faces.

Name: Pawmi
Flavor-Text: The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.

Name: Pawmo
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Pawmo uses a unique fighting technique in which it uses its forepaws to strike foes and zap them with electricity from its paw pads simultaneously.

Name: Pawmot
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Basic [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Pawmot’s fluffy fur acts as a battery. It can store the same amount of electricity as an electric car.

Name: Wattrel
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the United Wings attack.
Flavor-Text: When its wings catch the wind, the bones within produce electricity. This Pokémon dives into the ocean, catching prey by electrocuting them.

Name: Kilowattrel
Effects: This attack does 10 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the United Wings attack to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.

Name: Wigglytuff
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a Therapeutic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.

Name: Abra
Flavor-Text: Abra can teleport in its sleep. Apparently the more deeply Abra sleeps, the farther its teleportations go.

Name: Kadabra
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s telekinesis is immensely powerful. To prepare for evolution, Kadabra stores up psychic energy in the star on its forehead.

Name: Cleffa
Effects: Draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Because of its unusual, starlike silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

Name: Natu
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Because its wings aren’t yet fully grown, it has to hop to get around. It is always staring at something.

Name: Xatu
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a Basic [P] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: This odd Pokémon can see both the past and the future. It eyes the sun’s movement all day.

Name: Ralts
Flavor-Text: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people’s emotions.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

Name: Drifloon
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is whispered that any child who mistakes Drifloon for a balloon and holds on to it could wind up missing.

Name: Drifblim
Effects: Put 8 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: It can generate and release gas within its body. That’s how it can control the altitude of its drift.

Name: Mime Jr.
Effects: Your opponent chooses an attack from 1 of their Pokémon in play. Use the chosen attack as this attack.
Flavor-Text: In an attempt to confuse its enemy, it mimics the enemy’s movements. Then it wastes no time in making itself scarce!

Name: Spiritomb
Ability-Effect: Basic Pokémon V in play (both yours and your opponent’s) have no Abilities.
Effects: Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Its constant mischief and misdeeds resulted in it being bound to an Odd Keystone by a mysterious spell.

Name: Klefki
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, Basic Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) have no Abilities, except for Mischievous Lock.
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: In the past, noble families entrusted their vault keys to a Klefki. They passed the Klefki down through the generations, taking good care of it.

Name: Mimikyu
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon ex and Pokémon V.
Effects: Put 7 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon lives in dark places untouched by sunlight. When it appears before humans, it hides itself under a cloth that resembles a Pikachu.

Name: Dachsbun
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t be Burned. Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s [R] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The pleasant aroma that emanates from this Pokémon’s body helps wheat grow, so Dachsbun has been treasured by farming villages.

Name: Ceruledge
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon ex or Pokémon V, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The fiery blades on its arms burn fiercely with the lingering resentment of a sword wielder who fell before accomplishing their goal.

Name: Rabsca
Effects: Put a Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: The body that supports the ball barely moves. Therefore, it is thought that the true body of this Pokémon is actually inside the ball.

Name: Flittle
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Flittle’s toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon’s belly.

Name: Tinkatink
Effects: Put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal.

Name: Tinkatuff
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon will attack groups of Pawniard and Bisharp, gathering metal from them in order to create a large and sturdy hammer.

Name: Tinkaton
Ability-Effect: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 3 cards.
Effects: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This intelligent Pokémon has a very daring disposition. It knocks rocks into the sky with its hammer, aiming for flying Corviknight.

Name: Houndstone
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each [P] Pokémon in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: A lovingly mourned Pokémon was reborn as Houndstone. It doesn’t like anyone touching the protuberance atop its head.

Name: Mankey
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It is extremely quick to anger. It could be docile one moment, then thrashing away the next instant.

Name: Primeape
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Some researchers theorize that Primeape remains angry even when inside a Poké Ball.

Name: Annihilape
Effects: This attack does 70 damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It imbues its fists with the power of the rage that it kept hidden in its heart. Opponents struck by these imbued fists will be shattered to their core.

Name: Paldean Tauros
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This kind of Tauros, known as the Combat Breed, is distinguished by its thick, powerful muscles and its fierce disposition.

Name: Riolu
Effects: This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

Name: Lucario
Effects: If any of your [F] Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent’s last turn, this attack does 120 more damage.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Accelerating Stab.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras—even those of foes it could not otherwise see.

Name: Hawlucha
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may choose 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon and put 1 damage counter on each of them.
Flavor-Text: Its elegant finishing moves—performed by nimbly leaping around using its wings—are polished in the forest where it was born and raised.

Name: Nacli
Effects: Heal 20 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It was born in a layer of rock salt deep under the earth. This species was particularly treasured in the old days, as they would share precious salt.

Name: Naclstack
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon dry cures its prey by spraying salt over them. The curing process steals away the water in the prey’s body.

Name: Garganacl
Ability-Effect: During Pokémon Checkup, heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: Garganacl will rub its fingertips together and sprinkle injured Pokémon with salt. Even severe wounds will promptly heal afterward.

Name: Glimmet
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It absorbs nutrients from cave walls. The petals it wears are made of crystallized poison.

Name: Paldean Wooper
Effects: Search your deck for a Stadium card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: After losing a territorial struggle, Wooper began living on land. The Pokémon changed over time, developing a poisonous film to protect its body.

Name: Murkrow
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the United Wings attack.
Flavor-Text: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

Name: Sneasel
Flavor-Text: This cunning Pokémon hides under the cover of darkness, waiting to attack its prey.

Name: Weavile
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from your Bench to the Active Spot, you may switch in 1 of your opponent’s Benched Basic Pokémon to the Active Spot.
Flavor-Text: Evolution made it even more devious. It communicates by clawing signs in boulders.

Name: Sableye
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 70 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It dwells in the darkness of caves. It uses its sharp claws to dig up gems to nourish itself.

Name: Pawniard
Flavor-Text: Pawniard will fearlessly challenge even powerful foes. In a pinch, it will cling to opponents and pierce them with the blades all over its body.

Name: Bisharp
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon commands a group of several Pawniard. Groups that are defeated in territorial disputes are absorbed by the winning side.

Name: Kingambit
Ability-Effect: Your Basic Pokémon’s attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Only a Bisharp that stands above all others in its vast army can evolve into Kingambit.

Name: Mabosstiff
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch out your opponent’s Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Mabosstiff loves playing with children. Though usually gentle, it takes on an intimidating look when protecting its family.

Name: Shroodle
Effects: Put a Basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Though usually a mellow Pokémon, it will sink its sharp, poison-soaked front teeth into any that anger it, causing paralysis in the object of its ire.

Name: Grafaiai
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
You may discard as many Basic Energy cards as you like from your hand. This attack does 40 damage for each type of Basic Energy you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: The color of the poisonous saliva depends on what the Pokémon eats. Grafaiai covers its fingers in its saliva and draws patterns on trees in forests.

Name: Scizor
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each of your opponent’s Pokémon in play that has an Ability.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s pincers, which contain steel, can crush any hard object they get ahold of into bits.

Name: Varoom
Effects: Put up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.

Name: Revavroom
Ability-Effect: You must discard an Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders— now numbering eight—to generate energy.

Name: Noibat
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon emits ultrasonic waves from its large ears to search for fruit to eat. It mistakes Applin for its food.

Name: Cyclizar
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Apparently Cyclizar has been allowing people to ride on its back since ancient times. Depictions of this have been found in 10,000-year-old murals.

Name: Pidgey
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It is docile and prefers to avoid conflict. If disturbed, however, it can ferociously strike back.

Name: Pidgeotto
Flavor-Text: Very protective of its sprawling territorial area, this Pokémon will fiercely peck at any intruder.

Name: Jigglypuff
Effects: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: When its huge eyes waver, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

Name: Doduo
Effects: This Pokémon also does 10 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: The brains in its two heads appear to communicate emotions to each other with a telepathic power.

Name: Dodrio
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put 1 damage counter on this Pokémon. If you do, draw a card.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: An odd species that is rarely found. The three heads respectively represent joy, sadness, and anger.

Name: Ditto
Ability-Effect: Once during your first turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may search your deck and choose a Basic Pokémon you find there, except any Ditto. If you do, discard this Pokémon and all attached cards, and put the chosen Pokémon in its place. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its transformation ability is perfect. However, if made to laugh, it can’t maintain its disguise.

Name: Snorlax
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put up to 2 Leftovers cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s stomach is so strong, even eating moldy or rotten food will not affect it.

Name: Wingull
Flavor-Text: It soars high in the sky, riding on updrafts like a glider. It carries food tucked in its bill.

Name: Pelipper
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may choose 1: put a Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand; or search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, put it into your hand, and then shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It is a flying transporter that carries small Pokémon in its beak. It bobs on the waves to rest its wings.

Name: Skwovet
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may shuffle your hand and put it on the bottom of your deck. If you put any cards on the bottom of your deck in this way, draw a card.
Flavor-Text: No matter how much it stuffs its belly with food, it is always anxious about getting hungry again. So, it stashes berries in its cheeks and tail.

Name: Greedent
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon makes off with heaps of fallen berries by wrapping them in its tail, which is roughly twice the length of its body.

Name: Lechonk
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It searches for food all day. It possesses a keen sense of smell but doesn’t use it for anything other than foraging.

Name: Oinkologne
Effects: Flip 3 coins. Put a number of cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile into your hand.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon sends a flowerlike scent wafting about. Well-developed muscles in its legs allow it to leap more than 16 feet with no trouble at all.

Name: Tandemaus
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: Exhibiting great teamwork, they use their incisors to cut pieces out of any material that might be useful for a nest, then make off with them.

Name: Maushold
Effects: This attack does 70 damage for each of your Maushold in play.
Flavor-Text: The larger pair protects the little ones during battles. When facing strong opponents, the whole group will join the fight.

Name: Flamigo
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 3 Flamigo, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the United Wings attack.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon apparently ties the base of its neck into a knot so that energy stored in its belly does not escape from its beak.

Name: Forretress ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 5 Basic [G] Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck. If you searched your deck in this way, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Toedscruel ex
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks used by your opponent’s Pokémon done to all of your Pokémon that have Energy attached. (Existing effects are not removed. Damage is not an effect.)
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon that has any [G] Energy attached.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Espathra ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, attacks used by your opponent’s Active Pokémon cost [C] more.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to both Active Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Alakazam ex
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
This attack can be used even if this Pokémon is on the Bench.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mew ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 3 cards in your hand.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gardevoir ex
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic [P] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your [P] Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon. You can’t use this Ability on a Pokémon that would be Knocked Out.
Effects: This Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Glimmora ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can’t have more than 3 Benched Pokémon. If they have 4 or more Benched Pokémon, they discard Benched Pokémon until they have 3 Pokémon on the Bench. If more than one effect changes the number of Benched Pokémon allowed, use the smaller number.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Paldean Clodsire ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if a Stadium is in play, you may make your opponent’s Active Pokémon Poisoned.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Noivern ex
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.
During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Special Energy or Stadium cards from their hand.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Pidgeot ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. You can’t use more than 1 Quick Search Ability each turn.
Effects: You may discard a Stadium in play.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Wigglytuff ex
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, it gets +100 HP.
Effects: If you played a Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Squawkabilly ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your first turn, you may discard your hand and draw 6 cards. You can’t use more than 1 Squawk and Seize Ability during your turn.
Effects: Attach up to 2 Basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Wugtrio
Effects: Flip 3 coins. For each heads, discard the top 3 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: It has a vicious temperament, contrary to what its appearance may suggest. It wraps its long bodies around prey, then drags the prey into its den.

Name: Palafin
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If this Pokémon didn’t move from the Bench to the Active Spot this turn, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon’s ancient genes have awakened. It is now so extraordinarily strong that it can easily lift a cruise ship with one fin.

Name: Pawmi
Flavor-Text: The pads of its paws are electricity-discharging organs. Pawmi fires electricity from its forepaws while standing unsteadily on its hind legs.

Name: Clive
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you draw 2 cards for each Supporter card you find there.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Judge
Description: Each player shuffles their hand into their deck and draws 4 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Nemona
Description: Draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Paldean Student
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon that doesn’t have a Rule Box, reveal it, and put it into your hand. For each Paldean Student card (not including this card) in your discard pile, you may search for an additional Pokémon in this way. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Paldean Student
Description: Search your deck for a Pokémon that doesn’t have a Rule Box, reveal it, and put it into your hand. For each Paldean Student card (not including this card) in your discard pile, you may search for an additional Pokémon in this way. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Mew ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 3 cards in your hand.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gardevoir ex
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may attach a Basic [P] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your [P] Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon. You can’t use this Ability on a Pokémon that would be Knocked Out.
Effects: This Pokémon recovers from all Special Conditions.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Charizard ex
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 3 Basic [R] Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Tera-Rule: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).

Name: Arven
Description: Search your deck for an Item card and a Pokémon Tool card, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Clive
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you draw 2 cards for each Supporter card you find there.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Iono
Description: Each player shuffles their hand and puts it on the bottom of their deck. If either player put any cards on the bottom of their deck in this way, each player draws a card for each of their remaining Prize cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Nemona
Description: Draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Penny
Description: Put 1 of your Basic Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Wo-Chien ex
Effects: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Chi-Yu ex
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck.
Choose up to 3 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Basic [R] Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Chien-Pao ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may search your deck for up to 2 Basic [W] Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: You may discard any amount of [W] Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Miraidon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Basic [L] Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ting-Lu ex
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent’s Pokémon in play that have any damage counters on them have no Abilities, except for Pokémon ex.
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Koraidon ex
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach up to 2 Basic [F] Energy cards from your discard pile to your Basic [F] Pokémon in any way you like. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.

ex-Rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
